quotes: Bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure
Always bold: callout text per the callout specs
brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only)
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name

rules.intent: Maintain typographic hierarchy and readability by avoiding over-bolding of body copy.
rules.rule_text: Bold is strictly for hierarchy and key takeaways, never decoration. Within any single section, bolding must be restricted to headings, callout text, statistical callout numbers, at most one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and the first instance of the brand name in a benefit claim. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes, references, the generic name, or more than ~15% of the running body text in a section.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Prevent over-styled, visually jarring text blocks.
rules.rule_text: Never combine bolding, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis simultaneously on a single string, except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Choose at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Avoid confusion between hyperlinked and static text, and maintain clean styling guidelines for patient materials.
rules.rule_text: Do not underline any text unless it is a functioning hyperlink. Do not use italics for emphasis in patient-facing copy; reserve italics strictly for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
